extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds. The only transition that sets sent:=true while opted_out is true is t4 (on send_attempt if opted_out and acc==5). All other transitions either require not opted_out (t2, the only other way to set sent true) or set sent:=false (t1, t3, t5, t6, t7). So a violation requires reaching a state with opted_out=true and acc==5, then issuing send_attempt. However acc==5 is unreachable from the initial acc=0: each step transition (t5/t6/t7) computes (3*acc + k) mod 8 with k in {6,4}, both even. Since 3*acc preserves the parity of acc and adding an even constant and reducing mod 8 preserves parity, acc remains even forever. The reachable acc values from 0 are exactly {0,2,4,6}; 5 is odd and never reachable. optout (t1) does not modify acc, so this holds regardless of opt-out status. Therefore t4 is effectively dead code and can never fire. An exhaustive BFS over the full reachable state space (12 states, executions up to 10 events) confirms every reachable state satisfies opted_out implies not sent, with no acc==5 state ever reached. Property holds.
